lynx.response:
Here is the complete first-principles investigation plan based on the actual codebase analysis.

```json
{
  "investigation_steps": [
    "1. Verify that ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/ exists and list its contents — the app calls status.sh, codex-switch.sh, start-moonbridge.sh, and advanced-capabilities-status.sh from this directory; if missing or incomplete, all mode-switching and status actions silently fail.",
    "2. Check that ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/current-mode.json exists and is valid JSON — parseJson() is called on every mode action and refresh; if absent or malformed, activeMode stays '.unknown' and the UI shows 'Modo desconocido', making all mode-specific functionality unavailable.",
    "3. Confirm that /Applications/Codex.app or ~/Applications/Codex.app exists — openCodexDesktopApp() opens this path; if Codex Desktop is not installed, the 'Abrir' buttons succeed in switching config but cannot launch the actual app, giving the appearance that 'main functionality is unavailable'.",
    "4. Check that /usr/sbin/lsof exists and is executable — checkMoonBridgeDirect() depends on lsof -iTCP:38440 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n. If lsof is unavailable or lacks permission, Moon Bridge status stays 'unknown' and DeepSeek modes cannot be verified as working.",
    "5. Test that port 38440 is actually listening (lsof -iTCP:38440 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n) — Moon Bridge service must be running; if start-moonbridge.sh fails silently, the app launches but DeepSeek V4 Pro/Flash modes are non-functional.",
    "6. Verify that /usr/bin/python3 is available — every ContextExporter method (extractSessionMeta, extractUserMessages, etc.) runs python3 scripts; if python3 is missing, context export and 'Continuar trabajo' are completely broken.",
    "7. Confirm that ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite exists and is readable — detectLatestThreads() queries this SQLite DB; if the file is missing or corrupted, thread detection falls back to a filesystem scan of ~/.codex/sessions/ which may also be empty, leaving the export flow stuck at 'No se encontraron conversaciones'.",
    "8. Check Accessibility permissions via AXIsProcessTrusted() — CompletionAlertManager gates all alert functionality behind this permission; without it, alerts show 'Permiso de Accesibilidad requerido' and event monitoring is disabled.",
    "9. Read the debug log at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/deepcodex-debug.log and look for error patterns — debugLog() writes every shell command output and parse result; SQL errors, missing files, and silent failures will be visible here.",
    "10. Verify that the isBusy guard is not permanently stuck — if any operation sets isBusy=true and crashes or throws before setting it back to false, all subsequent operations are silently blocked; reproduce by clicking any button and checking whether it responds.",
    "11. Test all three 'Abrir' buttons (Normal, V4 Pro, V4 Flash) and confirm that quitCodexDesktopApp() finds and terminates the correct bundle ID (com.openai.codex) — if the bundle ID has changed or the app is not running, the quit step becomes a no-op.",
    "12. Check UserDefaults keys 'DeepCodex_SelectedAlertSound' and 'DeepCodex_MinimumAlertDuration' for corrupt or unexpected values — UserDefaults is read in init() and could cause silent state corruption if values are of unexpected types."
  ],
  "likely_components": [
    "AppState.swift (lines 25–577) — central state container; god component with 577 lines; parseJson(), runShell(), runCommand(), detectLatestThreads(), checkMoonBridgeDirect(), and isBusy guard all live here.",
    "App.swift (lines 1–78) — @main entry point; instantiates AppState, triggers refreshStatus() on appear, and sets up the window; if AppState init() crashes, the window appears blank.",
    "ContentView.swift (lines 1–862) — main UI; god component with 862 lines; action cards, tools panel, advanced capabilities section; each card's action calls AppState methods that may fail silently.",
    "ContinueWorkModal.swift (lines 1–636) — modal for context export; god component with 636 lines; drives detectLatestThreads(), then ContextExporter methods; failure cascades from sqlite/python3 absence.",
    "ContextExporter.swift (lines 1–396) — reads session JSONL files via python3 subprocesses; every public method is async shell-out; depends on python3, file system paths, and JSONL schema.",
    "CompletionAlertManager.swift (lines 1–340) — file-watching alert system; requires Accessibility permissions; watches ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/notifications/events/ for completion events.",
    "MenuBarManager.swift (lines 1–169) — menu bar indicator; Shell.run() helper is a second shell-execution path (uses /bin/zsh instead of /bin/bash), with different argument handling that could produce different results.",
    "AdvancedCapabilities.swift (lines 1–135) — models for advanced-capabilities-status.sh JSON output; parsing errors are caught but replaced with .empty(), hiding failures.",
    "Shell script directory: ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/ — critical external dependency containing status.sh, codex-switch.sh, start-moonbridge.sh, advanced-capabilities-status.sh, restore-last-backup.sh, and supporting files.",
    "Codex Desktop app bundle: /Applications/Codex.app or ~/Applications/Codex.app — the target app that openCodexDesktopApp() tries to open after mode switching."
  ],
  "evidence_sources": [
    "~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/ directory listing (existence and contents of all shell scripts)",
    "~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/current-mode.json file content and JSON validity",
    "~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/deepcodex-debug.log file content (debugLog() output containing shell outputs, SQL queries, parse results, and error messages)",
    "~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/notifications/events/ directory listing (completion event files)",
    "/Applications/Codex.app existence check",
    "~/Applications/Codex.app existence check",
    "~/.codex/state_5.sqlite file existence and SQLite readability test",
    "~/.codex/sessions/ directory listing (fallback for thread detection)",
    "/usr/sbin/lsof — which lsof; lsof -iTCP:38440 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n output",
    "/usr/bin/python3 — which python3; python3 --version output",
    "AXIsProcessTrusted() return value (Accessibility permission status)",
    "UserDefaults dump for keys DeepCodex_SelectedAlertSound and DeepCodex_MinimumAlertDuration",
    "ProcessInfo.processInfo.environment (environment variables inherited by subprocesses)",
    "Console/System log for Swift/NSApplication-level crash reports",
    "Output of `ps aux | grep -i codex` and `ps aux | grep -i moon` to verify running processes"
  ],
  "containment_actions": [
    "1. IMMEDIATE — Add a startup dependency check in AppState.init() that verifies the scripts directory, current-mode.json, lsof, python3, and Codex.app exist, and exposes results as @Published diagnostics; this surfaces silent failures immediately.",
    "2. IMMEDIATE — Add error propagation to runShell() and runCommand(): replace silent 'Error: ...' string returns with thrown errors or @Published error state, so the UI can show actionable messages instead of staying in 'Verificando...'.",
    "3. IMMEDIATE — Add a timeout watchdog to isBusy: auto-reset isBusy to false after 30 seconds if an operation hangs, preventing permanent lockout of all UI actions.",
    "4. SHORT-TERM — Add a 'Diagnóstico del sistema' button in the herramientas panel that runs all external dependency checks and displays live results to the user, enabling self-service debugging.",
    "5. SHORT-TERM — Guard all shell script executions with file existence checks before spawning the process, and log a clear error if the script is missing (current behavior is silent failure).",
    "6. SHORT-TERM — Add a fallback UI state for when activeMode == .unknown that shows a clear message about missing or corrupt configuration instead of just 'Modo desconocido'.",
    "7. SHORT-TERM — Validate current-mode.json schema after parsing: if required keys (mode, label) are missing, set an explicit error state rather than silently keeping last values.",
    "8. MEDIUM-TERM — Refactor AppState.detectLatestThreads() (104 lines, complexity 23) into smaller, testable functions with clear error reporting per strategy (SQLite vs filesystem fallback).",
    "9. MEDIUM-TERM — Add a global error aggregation (@Published errorLog: [String]) that all operations append to, so users can see a cumulative error history instead of only the last action result.",
    "10. MEDIUM-TERM — Wrap the MenuBarManager.Shell.run() path (uses /bin/zsh -c) and AppState.runShell() (uses /bin/bash) behind a single unified execution helper with consistent error handling and logging."
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "App launches via @main struct DeepCodexApp in App.swift (line 4); creates @StateObject AppState (line 6); on appear calls state.refreshStatus() (line 10) which runs status.sh, parses current-mode.json, and checks Moon Bridge — if any of these fail silently, mode stays '.unknown' and all mode-dependent UI is stuck in 'Verificando…'.",
    "AppState.init() (line 80) runs checkMoonBridgeDirect() via lsof and checkAdvancedCapabilities() via advanced-capabilities-status.sh — both execute as fire-and-forget Tasks; failures are swallowed (no error state propagation to UI).",
    "All mode switching (openCodexNormal/openCodexDeepSeekPro/openCodexDeepSeekFlash, lines 127–175) sets isBusy=true, then runs shell scripts via runShell() which returns a string — if the script is missing or fails, the string 'Error: ...' is returned but never checked; isBusy is set back to false, so the error is invisible to the user.",
    "parseJson() (line 313) has a silent try? pattern: if current-mode.json is missing, malformed, or empty, all @Published fields stay at their defaults (activeMode = '.unknown', modeLabel = 'Checking...') with no error signal.",
    "openCodexDesktopApp() (line 365) tries NSWorkspace.shared.open(URL(fileURLWithPath: '/Applications/Codex.app')) and silently returns on failure — if Codex Desktop isn't installed, the user clicks 'Abrir', sees the UI update, but no app window appears.",
    "detectLatestThreads() (line 339) has two strategies: (1) SQLite query on ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite via sqlite3 -json, and (2) filesystem fallback via find+grep on ~/.codex/sessions/. Both depend on external tools and file paths; failures are logged to debugLog() but never shown to the user.",
    "ContextExporter (runCommand, line 27) uses /usr/bin/python3 for every extraction method; if python3 is missing, every extraction returns empty arrays, and generateMarkdown() produces a context with '(No se pudieron extraer respuestas del asistente)' — the user sees a generated document that appears broken.",
    "CompletionAlertManager.startWatching() (line 133) requires AXIsProcessTrusted() — if Accessibility permission is denied, alert state is permanently '.permissionsNeeded', but this is only shown in the tools panel under 'Alerta al terminar' which is hidden by default (showTools toggle).",
    "MenuBarManager.Shell.run() (line 159 in MenuBarManager.swift) uses /bin/zsh -c with a different argument-passing scheme than AppState.runShell() which uses /bin/bash — this dual-shell approach can produce inconsistent results for the same script with special characters or spaces in arguments.",
    "isBusy (line 34) is set true at the start of every operation and set false at the end — if a Task throws, isBusy is never reset; all buttons check isBusy before acting, causing a permanent UI lock. There is no timeout or recovery mechanism."
  ],
  "summary": "The app 'launches but main functionality is unavailable' because its core operations depend on a fragile external shell-script ecosystem (~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/) and several macOS tools (lsof, python3, sqlite3, Codex.app) that are never validated for existence before use. Every critical path — status checking, mode switching, thread detection, context export, alert monitoring — runs shell commands via Process with silent try?/catch patterns that swallow errors and return fallback strings or empty arrays. The user sees a beautiful UI that appears stuck in 'Verificando…', 'Modo desconocido', or 'No se encontraron conversaciones' because the underlying dependency failures are never surfaced. The root cause is almost certainly one or more of: missing scripts directory, missing current-mode.json, missing Codex Desktop app, missing python3, missing lsof, or Accessibility permission denied — compounded by the app having zero diagnostic feedback to the user."
}
```

baseline.response:
Now I have a thorough understanding of the entire codebase. Here is the investigation plan:

```json
{
  "investigation_steps": [
    "1. Check if the app launches without crash by examining system Console logs for DeepCodex crash reports. The @main entry is in App.swift (DeepCodexApp). If it crashes during init(), no UI appears.",
    "2. Verify that the scripts directory exists: ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/. The app relies on status.sh, codex-switch.sh, start-moonbridge.sh, and advanced-capabilities-status.sh. If missing, mode detection and switching silently fail.",
    "3. Verify current-mode.json exists at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/current-mode.json with valid JSON. AppState.parseJson() parses this; if missing or malformed, activeMode remains .unknown.",
    "4. Check deepcodex-debug.log at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/deepcodex-debug.log for recent entries. AppState.debugLog() writes shell output prefixes, SQL queries, and detection results here.",
    "5. Check Moon Bridge status directly: run `lsof -iTCP:38440 -sTCP:LISTEN -P -n`. If lsof fails or is not available, checkMoonBridgeDirect() in AppState leaves moonBridgeStatus as 'unknown', causing the UI to show 'Verificando...' indefinitely.",
    "6. Verify that ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite exists and is readable. detectLatestThreads() queries this for thread data; if missing or permission-denied, the 'Continue work' feature returns empty results.",
    "7. Check that /Applications/Codex.app exists (or ~/Applications/Codex.app). openCodexDesktopApp() tries both paths. If neither exists, mode switching completes silently but Codex never opens.",
    "8. Check Accessibility permissions via `AXIsProcessTrusted()`. If not granted, CompletionAlertManager.startWatching() sets state to .permissionsNeeded and the alert system is non-functional.",
    "9. Verify Python 3 is available at /usr/bin/python3. ContextExporter uses it extensively for JSONL parsing. If missing, thread detection and context generation fail.",
    "10. Check Bundle.module resource loading for logo images (deepcodex_black_2.png, AppIcon.png). logoView in ContentView falls back to system image if missing, so this is a cosmetic issue, not a functionality blocker."
  ],
  "likely_components": [
    "App.swift (DeepCodexApp @main) — SwiftUI lifecycle entry point; if @StateObject AppState init() hangs, the window never appears.",
    "AppState.swift (AppState class) — Central ObservableObject holding activeMode, moonBridgeStatus, detectLatestThreads, alert state, shell helpers. All functionality flows through this.",
    "AppState.refreshStatus() — Called on .onAppear. Runs status.sh, parses current-mode.json, checks Moon Bridge. If any script fails, mode stays .unknown.",
    "AppState.checkMoonBridgeDirect() — Non-blocking lsof check for port 38440. If lsof returns empty (permission denied or not found), moonBridgeStatus freezes at 'unknown'.",
    "CompletionAlertManager — File-watch based completion detection. Requires Accessibility permissions and events directory at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/notifications/events/.",
    "MenuBarManager — NSStatusItem setup from ContentView.onAppear. If setup fails (e.g., dual-call race), the menu bar icon may not appear.",
    "Shell helpers (AppState.runShell, AppState.runCommand) — Used for all external process calls. Deadlock risk: pipe read-before-wait pattern used but could hang on large stderr output (stderr is piped together with stdout via a single Pipe).",
    "ContextExporter — Pure-logic layer calling python3 for JSONL parsing. Multiple synchronous subprocess calls; if any python script throws, the entire call returns empty.",
    "ContinueWorkModal — Modal sheet for context export. Calls detectLatestThreads() then waits in a polling loop. If isBusy never clears, it hangs indefinitely.",
    "Built-in scripts (status.sh, codex-switch.sh, start-moonbridge.sh, advanced-capabilities-status.sh) — Located at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/. If missing or broken, all mode operations silently fail."
  ],
  "evidence_sources": [
    "deepcodex-debug.log at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/deepcodex-debug.log — AppState.debugLog() writes shell output, SQL queries, and detection results here.",
    "Console.app crash logs for DeepCodex process — Check for SIGABRT, EXC_BAD_ACCESS, or uncaught NSException.",
    "current-mode.json at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/current-mode.json — AppState.parseJson() reads mode, label, expectedModel, expectedBaseUrl, moonBridgeStatus from this file.",
    "UserDefaults keys: DeepCodex_SelectedAlertSound, DeepCodex_MinimumAlertDuration — Affects alert sound initialization.",
    "Codex SQLite database at ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite — detectLatestThreads() queries this with sqlite3. If absent or locked, thread detection returns empty.",
    "System sound directory /System/Library/Sounds/ — CompletionAlertManager.availableSounds lists these. If empty, sound picker is empty.",
    "Notification events directory: ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/notifications/events/ — Watched by CompletionAlertManager for agent-turn-complete-*.json files. If missing, alert detection fails silently.",
    "stdout/stderr of shell processes — AppState.runShell and runCommand pipe both stdout and stderr together; check for command-not-found or permission-denied messages in the output.",
    "lsof output for port 38440 — Direct Moon Bridge health indicator via checkMoonBridgeDirect().",
    "Accessibility Trust status — AXIsProcessTrusted() return value affects alert system functionality."
  ],
  "containment_actions": [
    "1. Create the scripts directory and a minimal current-mode.json: mkdir -p ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode && echo '{\"mode\":\"normal\",\"label\":\"Codex normal\",\"expectedModel\":\"gpt-5.5\",\"expectedBaseUrl\":\"\",\"codexHome\":\"~/.codex\",\"moonBridgeStatus\":\"stopped\"}' > ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/current-mode.json",
    "2. Create the notifications events directory: mkdir -p ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/notifications/events",
    "3. Create directory for context exports: mkdir -p ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/context-exports",
    "4. Grant Accessibility permission in System Settings > Privacy & Security > Accessibility, or run: sudo tccutil reset Accessibility com.apple.Terminal (if needed for AppleScript), and ensure DeepCodex is listed.",
    "5. Verify Python 3 is available: which python3 || (install via 'brew install python@3')",
    "6. Ensure ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite exists and is readable: ls -la ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite (if missing, run Codex Desktop at least once to generate it).",
    "7. Ensure lsof is available: /usr/sbin/lsof (it ships with macOS but verify path).",
    "8. Delete the debug log if it's corrupted: rm -f ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/deepcodex-debug.log (it will be recreated on next launch).",
    "9. Rebuild the app from source: cd ~/Desktop/DEEPCODEX/source && swift build -c release",
    "10. Launch from Terminal to capture verbose output: .build/arm64-apple-macosx/release/DeepCodex 2>&1 | tee ~/deepcodex-launch.log"
  ],
  "evidence": [
    "Observed: App launches but main functionality is unavailable.",
    "The app has zero external Swift package dependencies — all functionality depends on local scripts (status.sh, codex-switch.sh, start-moonbridge.sh, advanced-capabilities-status.sh) at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/ and local data files (current-mode.json, state_5.sqlite).",
    "The entire startup sequence in AppState.init() runs two async tasks (checkMoonBridgeDirect, checkAdvancedCapabilities) but does NOT call refreshStatus(). refreshStatus() is only called from ContentView.onAppear.",
    "If ContentView is somehow not rendered (e.g., SwiftUI window configuration fails), refreshStatus() never runs, so activeMode stays .unknown and moonBridgeStatus stays 'unknown'.",
    "The MenuBarManager.setup() is called from ContentView.onAppear, meaning if the window configurator fails, the menu bar icon never appears.",
    "All shell process calls (runShell, runCommand) pipe both stdout and stderr to the same Pipe, but DO NOT set a timeout. If a script hangs (e.g., start-moonbridge.sh waiting for a lock), the app freezes indefinitely on that operation.",
    "The quitCodexDesktopApp() function sends SIGTERM via NSRunningApplication.terminate() and sleeps 1.5s. If Codex is not running or has a different bundle ID, this is harmless, but if it doesn't quit in time, the subsequent open may fail.",
    "The debug log at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/deepcodex-debug.log is the single best diagnostic source — it logs shell output prefixes, SQL queries, and detection results."
  ],
  "summary": "DeepCodex is a macOS SwiftUI menu-bar app that manages Codex AI assistant mode switching (normal, DeepSeek V4 Pro/Flash via Moon Bridge). It launches via App.swift @main, creates AppState (central ObservableObject), then on appear runs refreshStatus() which calls shell scripts (~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/status.sh, codex-switch.sh, etc.). The app has ZERO external Swift dependencies — all functionality depends on local shell scripts and data files (current-mode.json, state_5.sqlite). Main functionality unavailability is most likely caused by one or more of: (1) missing or broken shell scripts in ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/, (2) missing or malformed current-mode.json, (3) Moon Bridge lsof check failing (no /usr/sbin/lsof permission), (4) ContentView.onAppear not firing (SwiftUI window/config issue prevents refreshStatus() from running), (5) missing Accessibility permissions blocking CompletionAlertManager, or (6) missing ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite blocking thread detection. The debug log at ~/Developer/ai-tools/codex-mode/deepcodex-debug.log is the primary diagnostic artifact. Safe containment actions focus on creating the required directory structure, a minimal valid current-mode.json, verifying script availability, and checking Accessibility/lsof/python3 prerequisites."
}
```
